behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural MT output quality (grammar + semantic fidelity), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local logic; failure appears in model judgment. REAL-INFRA is not the core boundary; LLM-DEP is. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one model decision, not multiple agentic stages. Adversarial critique: if this were rewritten to only verify request routing to the MT endpoint, Agentic would be too high.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated multilingual evaluation set through the real translation API and score semantic preservation/fluency (e.g., human rubric or accepted automatic quality thresholds). Fail when outputs are literal but meaning-distorting, or grammatically broken despite successful API calls.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: API client error -> fallback translator -> caller response. In-process stubbing of a 503 is sufficient to expose this logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System because behavior text only requires handling a 503 condition, not real network failure semantics. Adversarial critique: if production failures are caused by real HTTP client timeout/connection behavior rather than mapped 503 responses, System may be needed.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub + SQLite-backed fallback. Force API stub to return 503 and assert: caller gets successful translated payload (from fallback), no propagated error, and fallback path telemetry/event is emitted.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on SQLite read path plus fallback translator wiring, not pure function logic. MOCK-MISS: unit-only tests of lookup logic can miss SQL/schema/query integration errors. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real external network/subprocess, no AI output dependency, no agentic chain. Rejected System because local SQLite interaction is covered at Integration per level definitions. Adversarial critique: if correctness depends on file locking/journal-mode behavior under real concurrent OS conditions, System might be required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase->translation rows, invoke fallback translator through service layer, and assert exact match for existing phrases plus expected normalization/casing behavior if specified.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation could be unit-tested, but behavior also requires persistence of the computed metric, creating a module boundary (calculator -> storage). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local storage, no model-quality dependency, no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about deterministic string-distance math and storage, not evaluating model judgment quality. Adversarial critique: if metric storage is asynchronous via external queue/service, System may become the true minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback string pairs with known edit-distance gold values, execute tracking pipeline end-to-end (compute + write), and assert persisted metric equals expected values for multiple edge cases (empty string, unicode chars, long strings).

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration across modules: primary selection + non-blocking background quality logging. Can be validated with deterministic stubs and local persistence/queue in-process. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real network/OS behavior, no dependence on model output quality content, and no multi-agentic chain. Rejected Workflow because no sequential model decisions are described; it is control-flow orchestration. Adversarial critique: if background logging reliability depends on real process boundaries/thread scheduling or external workers, System may be required.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators stubbed as available and distinct outputs. Assert synchronous response uses primary output, request latency is not blocked by comparison logging, and background task eventually writes the comparison metric/log record.
